page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server instance belonging to the c4d server family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 180.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of approximately 1.88 GB per vCPU, prioritizing raw processing power over memory capacity.

This instance does not include integrated local storage or graphics processing units, as the GPU count is zero and local storage size is listed as none. The absence of these bundled hardware components streamlines the resource profile, making it a highly specialized option for workloads that rely entirely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound execution.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high core density provide predictable, sustained performance for compute-heavy operations without the overhead of unused local storage or GPU hardware. The server is designed for workloads such as high-performance computing, intensive batch processing, and compute-heavy distributed systems where memory footprint requirements are modest.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 180.0 GB of RAM. With a memory-to-core ratio of 1.88 GB per vCPU, this instance is designed to maximize processing density for CPU-bound workloads. The hardware profile does not include bundled local storage or graphics accelerators, ensuring that resource allocation is focused entirely on dedicated compute capacity. This streamlined configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for high-performance computing tasks by eliminating the overhead of unused GPU or local disk resources. It is suited for compute-intensive workloads, batch processing, and distributed simulation tasks that require high-density CPU execution without massive memory or local storage demands.

tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and a streamlined memory profile for intensive processing workloads.

Zero bundled local storage and no GPU accelerators
Suited for batch processing and distributed computing
